news_items.headline: White Sox take UCLA SS Cholowsky with top pick
news_items.summary: The White Sox selected UCLA shortstop Roch Cholowsky on Saturday with the first pick of the MLB amateur draft.

news_items.headline: Day after cycle, White Sox OF Peters an All-Star
news_items.summary: White Sox outfielder Tristan Peters, who hit for the cycle Friday, was named to the American League All-Star team as an injury replacement Saturday.

news_items.headline: A's All-Star 1B Kurtz put on IL with thumb sprain
news_items.summary: Athletics slugger Nick Kurtz, who was set to start at first base for the American League at next week's All-Star Game, went on the 10-day injured list Saturday with a right thumb sprain.

news_items.headline: Red Sox plane issues force delayed start vs. Mets
news_items.summary: The Red Sox pulled out a 6-2 win Friday night despite arriving at Citi Field a little more than two hours before the scheduled first pitch after the team plane was grounded for nearly 24 hours due to mechanical issues.

news_items.headline: Sources: Cards' Wetherholt gets $112.5M deal
news_items.summary: Infielder JJ Wetherholt and the Cardinals are in agreement on an eight-year, $112.5 million contract extension, sources told ESPN.

news_items.headline: Ohtani (knee) scratched from start, to miss ASG
news_items.summary: Shohei Ohtani was scratched from his scheduled pitching start Friday due to continued irritation in his left knee and will skip the All-Star Game with the hope of ensuring he is "in a good place to be able to be in the rotation."

news_items.headline: Brewers put Harrison on IL with forearm tightness
news_items.summary: Milwaukee Brewers left-handed pitcher Kyle Harrison has gone on the 15-day injured list with tightness in his throwing forearm.

news_items.headline: Red Sox make shock pick at #20 in the first round of the MLB Draft: Jake Schaffner out of UNC
news_items.summary: Jake Schaffner, a speed demon of an infielder out of the University of North Carolina, was ranked just the 98th-best prospect in this draft by Baseball America. MLB Pipeline had him at #75. Keith Law of the The Athletic at #79. Craig Breslow, apparently, thinks he’s a lot better than that, as he made Schaffner […]
